Lines.FullText: Enumerations are immutable objects that encapsulate a value within a predefined set of named integral constants.

Elements.RawText: Enumerations are immutable objects that encapsulate a value within a predefined set of named integral constants.

Lines.FullText: These constants are predefined in an Enumeration type with the same name as the Enumeration.

Elements.RawText: These constants are predefined in an Enumeration type with the same name as the Enumeration.

Brief.RawText:
* @brief Enumerations are immutable objects that encapsulate a value within a predefined set of named integral constants.
 * These constants are predefined in an Enumeration type with the same name as the Enumeration.

Lines.FullText: When creating an Enumeration object, the Type Manager is used to validate the given enumerator value name against the

Elements.RawText: When creating an Enumeration object, the Type Manager is used to validate the given enumerator value name against the

Lines.FullText: Enumeration type stored within the Manager. If no type with the given Enumeration name is currently stored,

Elements.RawText: Enumeration type stored within the Manager. If no type with the given Enumeration name is currently stored,

Lines.FullText: construction of the Enumeration object will fail. Similarly, if the provided enumerator value name is not part of

Elements.RawText: construction of the Enumeration object will fail. Similarly, if the provided enumerator value name is not part of

Lines.FullText: the Enumeration type, the construction of the Enumeration object will also fail.

Elements.RawText: the Enumeration type, the construction of the Enumeration object will also fail.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * When creating an Enumeration object, the Type Manager is used to validate the given enumerator value name against the
 * Enumeration type stored within the Manager. If no type with the given Enumeration name is currently stored,
 * construction of the Enumeration object will fail. Similarly, if the provided enumerator value name is not part of
 * the Enumeration type, the construction of the Enumeration object will also fail.

Lines.FullText: Since the Enumerations objects are immutable the value of an existing Enumeration object cannot be modified.

Elements.RawText: Since the Enumerations objects are immutable the value of an existing Enumeration object cannot be modified.

Lines.FullText: However, the Enumeration object encapsulated by a smart pointer of the corresponding type can be replaced

Elements.RawText: However, the Enumeration object encapsulated by a smart pointer of the corresponding type can be replaced

Lines.FullText: with a newly created one. This replacement is accomplished using the assignment operator with the right

Elements.RawText: with a newly created one. This replacement is accomplished using the assignment operator with the right

Lines.FullText: operand being a constant string literal containing the enumerator value name valid for the Enumeration type

Elements.RawText: operand being a constant string literal containing the enumerator value name valid for the Enumeration type

Lines.FullText: of the original Enumeration object.

Elements.RawText: of the original Enumeration object.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * Since the Enumerations objects are immutable the value of an existing Enumeration object cannot be modified.
 * However, the Enumeration object encapsulated by a smart pointer of the corresponding type can be replaced
 * with a newly created one. This replacement is accomplished using the assignment operator with the right
 * operand being a constant string literal containing the enumerator value name valid for the Enumeration type
 * of the original Enumeration object.

Lines.FullText: The Enumeration types are stored within a Type Manager. In any given Instance of openDAQ, a single Type Manager should

Elements.RawText: The Enumeration types are stored within a Type Manager. In any given Instance of openDAQ, a single Type Manager should

Lines.FullText: exist that is part of its Context.

Elements.RawText: exist that is part of its Context.

Description.RawText:
*
 * The Enumeration types are stored within a Type Manager. In any given Instance of openDAQ, a single Type Manager should
 * exist that is part of its Context.
